title: streptanthus glandulosus subsp. glandulosus
intro: Streptanthus glandulosus subsp. glandulosus, also known as the Sierra bruceanth or Sierra jewelflower, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Sierra Nevada mountain range in California. It is a member of the broccoli family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and acting as an antioxidant, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other remedies.
